rationale: Add a small procedural config to cap CLI argument size via a local guard, reducing risk of Windows command-line length failures without touching code paths.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.arg_guard.v1",
  "max_cli_arg_chars": 6000,
  "max_inline_payload_chars": 4000,
  "prefer_tempfile_over_inline": true,
  "notes": "Heuristic limits for Windows cmd/ps argument length; keep payloads short to avoid spawn failures."
}

risks: Downstream components may ignore this config if not yet wired; no functional change unless read by the executor.
expected_benefit: Provides a single source of truth for safe CLI argument limits to prevent intermittent Windows spawn failures when payloads are large.
